agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-sale lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity through deferred reservation, mutual exclusivity of discount mechanisms, order immutability after payment, and time-bound refund eligibility.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout (with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions, and Returns)","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Adding items to the cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-limited hold on inventory units that begins when checkout is initiated. Prevents other customers from purchasing the same units during the checkout window."},{"term":"Checkout Abandonment","definition":"The condition when a customer initiates checkout but does not complete payment within the 15-minute reservation window. Triggers automatic release of all reserved inventory."},{"term":"Active Promotion","definition":"A currently-in-effect price reduction applied to a specific product. A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-supplied code that applies a discount at the order level. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing on the same order."},{"term":"Discount Mechanism","definition":"Any source of price reduction — either a discount code or a promotional price. Only one mechanism may apply per order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmation","definition":"The irreversible event marking an order as paid. After this point the order becomes immutable."},{"term":"Order Immutability","definition":"The invariant that no modifications (item changes, quantity adjustments, address edits) are permitted once payment is confirmed."},{"term":"Zero-Inventory Product","definition":"A product whose available inventory count is zero. It may remain visible in the catalog but cannot be added to a checkout or purchased."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-calendar-day period starting from confirmed delivery during which a customer may request a refund."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to the cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory reservation occurs only when checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If inventory were reserved at cart-add time, the system would allow phantom stock locks, corrupting inventory counts and blocking legitimate purchases. Deferred reservation is a data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"After a customer adds an item to their cart, the product's available inventory count remains unchanged."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Inventory reservations expire and are automatically released if payment is not completed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy threshold. It could be changed to 10 or 20 minutes by business decision without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A reservation created at time T that has no corresponding payment confirmation by T+15 minutes is released, and the reserved units are returned to available inventory."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on a single product create ambiguous pricing — the system cannot deterministically resolve which price to display or charge. This is a state-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to activate a second promotion on a product that already has an active promotion is rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive per order. Only one discount mechanism may apply.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing both would result in double-discounting, leading to incorrect order totals and potential revenue loss. The order pricing calculation must be deterministic and singular.","testable_assertion":"An order that has a promotional price applied rejects application of a discount code, and vice versa."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification would break financial reconciliation, fulfillment pipeline integrity, and audit trails. This is a hard state-transition constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any mutation request (item change, quantity change, address change, cancellation) on an order in 'paid' or later status is rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling inventory that does not exist creates fulfillment obligations that cannot be met — a physical constraint that corrupts the order pipeline.","testable_assertion":"Checkout initiation or inventory reservation for a product with available_inventory == 0 is rejected with an out-of-stock error."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 calendar days of confirmed delivery.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a customer-service policy. It could be extended for VIP customers or holiday seasons without breaking system integrity.","testable_assertion":"A refund request submitted on day 31 or later after delivery confirmation is rejected. A request on day 30 or earlier is accepted for processing."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart and available inventory exists","when":"The customer adds another unit of the same product to the cart","then":"The product's available inventory count is unchanged; no reservation is created"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with items in the cart","when":"Checkout begins","then":"Inventory is reserved for each item in the cart, reducing available inventory by the corresponding quantities"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer has initiated checkout and inventory is reserved","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment confirmation","then":"All inventory reservations for that checkout session are released and available inventory is restored"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A customer has initiated checkout and inventory is reserved","when":"Payment is confirmed within 15 minutes","then":"The reservation is converted to a confirmed allocation; the order is created in 'paid' status"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An administrator attempts to activate a second promotion on the same product","then":"The system rejects the second promotion with an error indicating a promotion is already active"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer's cart contains a product with an active promotional price","when":"The customer attempts to apply a discount code at checkout","then":"The system rejects the discount code, indicating it cannot be combined with promotional pricing"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer applies a valid discount code at checkout","when":"The order total is calculated","then":"Only the discount code reduction is applied; no promotional pricing is layered on top"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order has been paid and confirmed","when":"The customer or an admin attempts to modify the order (items, quantities, address)","then":"The modification is rejected with an error stating the order is immutable after payment"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A product is listed in the catalog with zero available inventory","when":"A customer attempts to initiate checkout with that product","then":"Checkout is blocked and the customer is informed the product is out of stock"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered 25 days ago","when":"The customer submits a refund request","then":"The refund request is accepted and enters the refund processing workflow"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An order was delivered 31 days ago","when":"The customer submits a refund request","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message that the 30-day refund window has expired"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A customer is in checkout with a promotional-priced item","when":"The customer enters a discount code before the promotional price is applied","then":"The system applies whichever mechanism was requested but prevents the second from being added (per escalation — precedence rule TBD)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Inventory drops to zero after cart-add but before checkout initiation","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation fails with an out-of-stock error for the affected product. The customer is informed and may remove the item to proceed with remaining items."},{"scenario":"Two customers attempt to reserve the last unit simultaneously","expected_behavior":"Only one reservation succeeds (first-write-wins). The second customer receives an out-of-stock error at checkout initiation."},{"scenario":"Customer has both a discount code and a promotional item in cart","expected_behavior":"System must enforce mutual exclusivity. The precedence/resolution strategy is ambiguous in the spec (see escalation). One mechanism must be chosen; the other is rejected."},{"scenario":"Payment gateway times out at minute 14 of the 15-minute window","expected_behavior":"If payment confirmation is not received by minute 15, reservation is released regardless of in-flight payment attempts. System must handle the race condition between late payment confirmation and reservation expiry."},{"scenario":"Product inventory becomes zero between reservation and payment confirmation","expected_behavior":"The reservation already holds the units — the customer's reserved inventory is protected. Zero available inventory only blocks NEW reservations."},{"scenario":"Refund requested on exact day 30 boundary","expected_behavior":"The request is accepted. Day 30 is inclusive of the refund window (delivery_date + 30 days)."},{"scenario":"Promotion is deactivated while customer is mid-checkout","expected_behavior":"The promotional price that was applied at checkout initiation should be honored for the active checkout session. The price was locked when checkout began."},{"scenario":"Order delivered but delivery date is disputed by the customer","expected_behavior":"The 30-day refund window is anchored to confirmed delivery date in the system. Dispute resolution for delivery date is a separate customer service process."},{"scenario":"Discount code entered on an order containing both promotional and non-promotional items","expected_behavior":"Ambiguous — does mutual exclusivity apply per-line-item or per-order? The spec says 'per order,' so the entire order is blocked from using the discount code if any item has a promotional price."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Deferred reservation prevents phantom stock locks that would otherwise block real sales. Mutual exclusivity of discounts prevents double-discounting and margin erosion. Order immutability after payment ensures financial reconciliation accuracy.","operational":"15-minute reservation timeout keeps inventory flowing and prevents checkout-hoarding. Single-promotion-per-product simplifies pricing engine and prevents ambiguous catalog displays. Zero-inventory purchase block prevents fulfillment of unshippable orders.","user":"Customers may experience friction if promotional items block their discount codes — clear messaging is essential. The 15-minute checkout window is tight for complex orders. Refund window clarity builds trust.","risk":"Race condition between reservation expiry and late payment is a critical technical risk that could lead to overselling or lost revenue. Ambiguity in discount precedence could lead to customer complaints or revenue leakage depending on implementation choice.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Checkout time pressure (15 min), inability to stack discounts, clear refund window expectations"},{"group":"Product/Marketing Team","impact":"Single-promotion constraint limits campaign flexibility; must deactivate existing promo before launching new one"},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability and single-discount-mechanism simplify reconciliation and audit; refund window bounds liability"},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Zero-inventory blocking and proper reservation lifecycle prevent unshippable orders from entering the pipeline"},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle edge cases around refund window boundaries, discount mutual exclusivity complaints, and checkout timeout frustrations"}]},"confidence":{"level":55,"reasoning":"The specification is well-articulated for most rules with clear boundaries and testable conditions. However, there is a meaningful ambiguity around discount mechanism precedence when both a promotional price and a discount code could apply — the spec says they are mutually exclusive but does not define which wins or whether the customer chooses. This prevents full confidence in acceptance criteria for that interaction.","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory reservation lifecycle (deferred reservation, 15-min timeout, release)","Order immutability after payment confirmation","Zero-inventory purchase blocking","Single active promotion per product constraint","30-day refund window from delivery"],"low_confidence_areas":["Discount precedence: when a customer has both a promotional item and a discount code, which mechanism takes priority — or does the customer choose?","Whether the promotional price lock occurs at cart-add or checkout-initiation time","Whether the refund window is calculated in calendar days or business days (assumed calendar)","Boundary behavior: is 'within 30 days' inclusive of day 30 (assumed yes)"],"assumptions":["Refund window of 30 days means 30 calendar days, inclusive of day 30","Promotional price is locked at checkout initiation, not at cart-add time","Mutual exclusivity of discounts is enforced at the order level, not the line-item level","Inventory reservation is an atomic operation with optimistic or pessimistic concurrency control"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"When a customer's order contains a product with an active promotional price AND the customer enters a valid discount code, the spec states they cannot be combined but does not specify the resolution strategy. Options: (A) promotional pricing always wins, (B) discount code always wins, (C) the better deal for the customer wins, (D) the customer is asked to choose. This affects pricing logic, UX flows, and customer satisfaction.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Product/Marketing Team","Finance/Accounting","Engineering"],"options":["A) Promotional pricing takes precedence — discount code is rejected","B) Discount code takes precedence — promotional pricing is overridden","C) System applies whichever yields the greater discount for the customer","D) Customer is presented with a choice at checkout"],"recommendation":"Option D (customer chooses) provides the best UX and transparency, but Option C (best deal for customer) is simplest to implement and least likely to generate support tickets. Recommend clarifying with product team before implementation."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-sale lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity through deferred reservation, mutual exclusivity of discount mechanisms, order immutability after payment, and time-bound refund eligibility.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout (with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions, and Returns)","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient colle

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this e-commerce domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me check for any issues:

1. Cart additions don't reserve inventory - clear
2. Inventory reserved at checkout start - clear
3. 15-minute abandonment timeout - clear
4. Max one active promotion per product - clear
5. Discount codes can't combine with promotional pricing - clear
6. Orders immutable after payment confirmed - clear
7. Zero inventory = can't purchase even if listed - clear
8. Refunds within 30 days of delivery - clear

No contradictions. No critical missing context. 

Now I'm spotting some genuine ambiguities worth flagging. The discount mechanism rule doesn't specify which takes precedence when both a promotion and discount code could apply to the same order. There's also the question of what happens to refund eligibility if an item never arrives, and whether inventory dropping to zero mid-checkout after reservation creates a conflict. The discount precedence issue is the most concrete ambiguity here.
